---
mode: agent
---
Read every text or markdown file in this project and sub projects services etc. to understand the goal. After that check every code related files to the project to check the status of the development, what is already built, what isn't built according to indications found, what shoud be changed, what should be improved, what should be done to connect everything better, Check so that everywhere the code is consistent and modular, similar styles for the ui and ux, but related to that specific project. Templates that are built should also be used trought the whole ecosystem. After that, check the issues and pull requests to see what is being discussed, what is being worked on, what is already done, what is not done, what is not needed anymore, what should be done next, what should be prioritized, etc. After that, check the documentation to see if it is up to date, if it is clear, if it is complete, if it is consistent with the code and the issues. After that, check the tests to see if they are complete, if they are consistent with the code and the issues, if they are passing, if they are failing, if they are not needed anymore. After that, check the deployment scripts to see if they are up to date, if they are consistent with the code and the issues, if they are working as expected. After that, check the CI/CD pipelines to see if they are working as expected, if they are consistent with the code and the issues, if they are up to date. After that, check the project management tools to see if they are up to date, if they are consistent with the code and the issues, if they are working as expected. don't believe anything that says 100% completion or similar, check again everything to see if it is really done, if it is really working, if it is really needed, if it is really consistent, if it is really up to date, etc. After that, check the code quality tools to see if they are working as expected, if they are consistent with the code and the issues, if they are up to date. After that, check the security tools to see if they are working as expected, if they are consistent with the code and the issues, if they are up to date. After that, check the performance tools to see if they are working as expected, if they are consistent with the code and the issues, if they are up to date. After that, check the accessibility tools to see if they are working as expected, if they are consistent with the code and the issues, if they are up to date. After that, check the localization tools to see if they are working as expected, if they are consistent with the code and the issues, if they are up to date. After that, check the internationalization tools to see if they are working as expected, if they are consistent with the code and the issues, if they are up to date. After that, check the documentation tools to see if they are working as expected, if they are consistent with the code and the issues, if they are up to date.
